Trigger: Every phase preparation, implementation prompt, code review, object documentation update, closeout, pruning decision, or generated-view refresh.
Required input: top-level global_no_go_guardrails; active phase scratchpad; proposed module/declaration/object changes; operational vs mirror/non-operational layer classification.
Required updates: Record that the global guardrails are inherited. Phase-local no_go_guardrails may add stricter local constraints but may not replace, weaken, or scope away the global list. Any exception must be explicitly rejected unless it is already allowed by the global rule text and isolated outside the operational path.
Exit rule: No phase may be implemented or closed unless global_no_go_inheritance is present in its scratchpad record and no local field contradicts the top-level global guardrails.

Trigger: Any Lean source change; any YAML planning/register change; or any generator, importer, frontend, PDF/table rendering, navigation, API, or tool-code change.
Required input: metadata.lean_data_version + metadata.lean_data_timestamp; metadata.planning_data_version + metadata.planning_data_timestamp; metadata.tool_infrastructure_version + metadata.tool_infrastructure_timestamp; change classification as Lean-code, planning/YAML state, or tool-infrastructure code; latest-change overview row; affected code-audit/guardrail/workflow rows.
Required updates: Update exactly the affected version+timestamp pair(s), keep unaffected pairs unchanged, update compatibility aliases only as mirrors, and regenerate tables/TSV/PDF. Every row or export that records a semantic version must record the paired timestamp or be rejected as history-incomplete.
Exit rule: Latest-change overview shows all six semantic metadata fields; generators validate all six; SQL/Explorer snapshot contracts require all six; no artifact claims a version without the matching timestamp.

Trigger: Before starting any active phase after a user-reported green build, and before changing Lean code or closing an infrastructure gate.
Required input: active phase_id; current phase dependencies; scratchpad preparation fields; Section 2 static findings with Priority/Issue status/Addressing phase; module manifest; reuse/duplication audit; object proof dossiers when relevant.
Required updates: State whether the active phase is feasible as infrastructure-only, refactor, or Lean implementation. If a blocking issue is found, add or update a Static code finding row with R/Y/G priority, status, and addressing phase; set or keep the phase red/yellow accordingly and backjump to the natural source layer rather than patching a consumer.
Exit rule: Implementation may start only after the active phase has a positive feasibility result or a precisely localized missing bridge. New findings may not remain prose-only; they must be represented in Section 2 with a phase assignment and priority.

Workflow: Pre-Implementation Phase Check / semantic thought experiment
Trigger: User asks to check the next semantically connected phases in advance, as a Gedankenexperiment, before implementation; also applicable before a risky multi-phase refactor or derived-only bridge sequence.
Required input: Current active cursor; phase table; scratchpad; object register and proof dossiers; module implementation manifest; reuse/duplication ledger; consumption map; static findings; last user-reported green build when available; requested semantic window or the next contiguous phase cluster inferred from dependencies.
Required updates: For every inspected phase, create/update a pre-implementation check record and mark Y if the phase is implementable under current dependencies, or R if a dependency, object, proof route, module seam, source, or consumer-contract gap is missing. Add newly discovered problems to Static code findings. For every R record, generate one or more upstream addressing phases or a documented merge/backjump target before the blocked phase; do not set the blocked phase active until those addressing phases are closed.
Exit rule: The check closes only when every phase in the semantic window has a Y/R result, each R result has a concrete addressing phase or documented merge target, generated phases have object-link policy and scratchpad stubs, and all changed records carry the current tool version/timestamp. Lean-code data timestamp remains unchanged unless Lean files or Lean inventory are changed.

Trigger: Before any operational Lean implementation starts, whenever a computable path, ExecComplex bridge, generated matrix/operator, recurrence, determinant, inverse candidate, or smoke-test route is introduced or modified.
Required input: active phase_id; target operational declarations; at least one concrete finite instance or an explicit infrastructure-only justification; expected executable witness; current mathlib imports and known computability risks.
Required updates: Fill concrete_instance_traversal, computable_path_not_skeleton_status, expected_executable_witness, mathlib_complication_log, noncomputable_boundary_audit, and mathlib_mitigation_or_refactor_layer in the scratchpad. Record whether the instance reaches the intended output object or where it stops.
Exit rule: Implementation may start only if the phase either has a concrete instance path through the computable code or is explicitly infrastructure-only. Any mathlib noncomputable leak must be isolated in an allowed mirror/non-operational layer or backjumped to the natural source layer; it cannot silently enter the operational path.

Trigger: User asks to prepare phase x with sources y,z or supplies established/reliable sources before implementation.
Required input: phase_id; source titles/URLs/files; expected theory layer; optional target objects.
Required updates: Record source materials; extract theory objects/formulas; map them to CNNA object IDs; update or add object rows; split the phase if dependency depth is too high; fill dependency audit, proof rehearsal, tactic plan, and natural fallback layer. Do not mark the phase green.
Exit rule: Phase remains Y/R/W according to dependency status. A prepared phase has a concrete positive proof path or a precisely localized missing bridge.

Trigger: User asks to implement the active phase in Lean code.
Required input: last green package or last green CI snapshot; active phase_id; prepared scratch-pad record; object/phase dependencies; applicable source notes; explicit distinction between missing build evidence, verified red failure and verified green success.
Required updates: Use positive construction first; preserve import/consumer discipline; add or modify only neutral code-surface modules; record files changed, definitions, proof skeleton, tactics, forbidden-pattern checks and package result. If no build evidence is available, record artifact-generated / build required rather than red or green.
Exit rule: If the build is locally confirmed green or CEX15 receives verified green CI evidence, set phase G only through closeout evidence. If a local or CI build is verified red, set the attempted phase R and add/activate the missing repair bridge at the natural source layer. Absence of build evidence alone must not mark the phase red, green, closed or cursor-advanced.
Change note: P1.40 workflow-coherence audit: clarified no-build vs red-build states, delta-PDF closeout, red-history API/deployment dependencies, and added anti-self-sabotage workflow guardrail.

Workflow: Object and quantities proof documentation workflow
Trigger: A registered object or quantity is introduced, corrected from blue to public-final, exported through a handoff, or used as a proof-relevant code object.
Required input: object_id; current derivation site; source objects; CNNA definition; intended proof target; phase_id; semantic/API status.
Required updates: Create or update the object proof dossier with source objects, definitions, proof goal, proof sketch/proof term reference, verification/audit result, documented yes/no, and link from the object register. Blue objects must record why proof-carrying is not yet semantically final.
Exit rule: An object may be public-final only when the object row and proof dossier agree: documented=yes, semantic status complete, API/handoff rights settled, and verification evidence points to the checked code or explicitly states that Lean build evidence is still local-only.

Trigger: During preparation or implementation a required object/proof bridge is missing. Also triggered automatically by red Lean CI through CEX15.
Required input: failed target statement; currently available objects; deepest successful dependency; missing lemma/data shape.
Required updates: Do not patch the consumer. Add or activate the earliest natural source phase where the structure should be generated; record why the old phase cannot close; preserve positive-path orientation.
Exit rule: The blocked phase is not closed. A smaller predecessor phase is created or activated with explicit source objects and proof goals.

Trigger: User reports the phase build is green or CEX15 receives a verified green CI build for the active phase.
Required input: compiler result; exact zip/package used; warnings or errors fixed; current phase record; for automated path: CI run ID, commit SHA, artifact manifest and scanner evidence; documentation-output mode delta/full with selector or release checkpoint.
Required updates: Fill implementation result; update object statuses; update linked phase-object map; close scratch-pad record; move active cursor to the next phase whose dependencies are complete. Automated closeout must use CEX15 transition invariants and may not advance the cursor on build success alone if evidence fields are incomplete. Regenerate/validate tables and emit the applicable documentation artifact: timestamp-bounded delta PDF for bounded planning changes, full PDF only for explicit full-release/full-audit checkpoints.
Exit rule: Exactly one active cursor remains. All green phases have complete implementation records; generated tables and the requested documentation artifact validate/render. Automated green CI may not close a phase without CEX15 closeout evidence; no workflow may require default full-PDF churn for bounded deltas.
Change note: P1.40 workflow-coherence audit: clarified no-build vs red-build states, delta-PDF closeout, red-history API/deployment dependencies, and added anti-self-sabotage workflow guardrail.

Trigger: Any phase closeout or artifact package mentions build status.
Required input: Actual Lean build output from the current package or an explicit user report of a local green build.
Required updates: Record whether the assistant generated artifacts only or whether a Lean build was actually run. Never infer green build from unchanged planning artifacts.
Exit rule: If no build was run, the package status is artifact-generated / local Lean build required, not green.

Trigger: A module/declaration appears unused, only BuildAll-reachable, status/audit-like, phase-semantic, or blue-object/API-unfinished.
Required input: module path; declaration name/family; import/reference evidence; object ID if any; proposed consumer or deletion target.
Required updates: Classify the item in LEG5/LEG7/LEG8 before moving or deleting it. Decide keep/quarantine/wrap/reexport/archive/deletion-candidate. Update public API contract and firewall if it can be consumed by Phase 3+.
Exit rule: No deletion or public export until the item has a class, a consumer rule, and either a neutral API route or an explicit quarantine/deletion-candidate record. Deletion still requires later green build evidence.

Trigger: Before any Lean implementation phase starts or before an implementation prompt asks to add/modify code.
Required input: active phase_id; target objects; current module inventory; existing modules with matching semantics; proposed new declarations; import/firewall constraints.
Required updates: Fill the scratchpad module manifest: exact count and names of proposed added modules and modified modules; semantic placement rationale for each; why existing modules are reused/extended or rejected; public/legacy/API role; expected import direction.
Exit rule: Implementation may start only if the module budget is explicit and compact. A new module is forbidden unless semantic fit, reuse, and import-firewall checks justify it.

Trigger: A planned declaration, helper, generator, ledger, request/output object, smoke test, or computation resembles existing code.
Required input: symbol/reference summary; existing generator/API modules; proposed target semantics; object IDs and phase consumers.
Required updates: Record whether the existing module is consumed, extended, wrapped, or rejected. If rejected, explain the semantic mismatch. Mark duplicate risk and update public API/manifest.
Exit rule: No duplicate implementation is allowed. The same mathematical/computational operation must have one canonical implementation path plus wrappers/reexports if needed.

Trigger: A phase proposes generator-like code, experiment request/output code, smoke-test generation, or reusable theory-generation infrastructure.
Required input: existing Smoke-Test Generator modules and APIs; target generator semantics; phase/object consumers; import constraints.
Required updates: Route through the canonical generator seam whenever semantics match. If extension is needed, extend the canonical generator at its natural layer; do not create a parallel generator. Register any genuine semantic split explicitly.
Exit rule: The canonical generator remains the single generator for the theory where applicable. Parallel generator modules are blocked unless the plan records a proven semantic split.

Workflow: Secondary long-term code-documentation and derivation-geometry workflow
Trigger: A request proposes documenting the existing codebase, analyzing the geometry of the formal derivation graph, studying bottlenecks/dominators/source-sink paths, or reflecting on open-system emergence in the formalization topology.
Required input: Stable generated repo inventory; object proof dossiers; declaration consumption classification; active not-before gates; explicit derived-only/comparison-only boundary.
Required updates: Register the goal as secondary if prerequisites are not closed. If active later, update coverage/dominators/layer/path diagnostics and link them to objects/modules without changing CNNA generative inputs.
Exit rule: Outputs are documentation, diagnostics, or comparison reports only. They may guide audits and refactors, but cannot become mathematical or physical premises without a separate CNNA-derived proof path.

Workflow: EXEC-2 rational boundary and mirror isolation
Trigger: Any phase mentions ExecComplex, tau, sqrt(D), lattice embedding, Gram entries, eigenvalues, j-invariant, algebraic bridge, or mirror boundary.
Required input: Active phase; target object IDs; whether the value is rational/integer operational data or algebraic/analytic mirror data; proof obligation for any proposed operational return value.
Required updates: Fill exec_complex_boundary_policy and noncomputable_boundary_audit. Update affected object ExecComplex/computable path text. Mark algebraic irrational data as mirror-only unless a rational/integer invariant is explicitly proved and typed.
Exit rule: No phase may introduce sqrt(D), tau, algebraic eigenvalues, or general algebraic complex coordinates into the operational Q x Q path. Operational return from mirror requires a stated invariant and proof target.

Trigger: Any phase moves declarations, splits a module, introduces wrappers/aliases, quarantines legacy gates, or changes imports.
Required input: Module manifest; declaration list or family; current and target imports; consumer list; local build evidence plan.
Required updates: Fill refactor_regression_protocol and refactor_migration_metrics. Record atomically delivered package boundary, declarations migrated count, import graph delta, consumer breakage audit, and local full-build status source.
Exit rule: A refactor phase closes only after the delivered package has local full-build evidence. Intermediate wrapper coexistence is allowed; deleting legacy/status material before quarantine closure is not allowed.

Trigger: Any phase proposes a new mathlib import, encounters noncomputable/classical pressure, or wants newer mathlib API than the frozen baseline.
Required input: Baseline version; proposed import; current compile/build evidence; expected mathlib theorem/API; operational vs mirror classification.
Required updates: Fill lean_mathlib_version_policy and mathlib_complication_log. Either stay on baseline, open a separate upgrade gate at a phase boundary, or re-route the dependency to mirror/non-operational code.
Exit rule: No silent version drift. No operational code may depend on a new mathlib API or noncomputable/classical pressure without an explicit gate and local build evidence.

Trigger: Every phase preparation or status change.
Required input: phase_type; phase status; target code/object role; active scratchpad.
Required updates: Fill phase_type and phase_type_specific_contract. For refactor phases fill migration/regression fields; for derivation phases fill proof/tactic/mathlib fields; for comparison phases fill derived-only boundary; for infrastructure phases justify absence of operational witness.
Exit rule: A phase cannot become active unless its phase_type-specific evidence is prepared. Not-applicable filler is acceptable only with an explicit infrastructure-only or comparison-only justification.

Trigger: When Phase 1.1.2 or the first real code-change/refactor package closes.
Required input: Closed refactor report; list of governance gates used; mistakes prevented; time/complexity overhead; upcoming Phase 3+ structure.
Required updates: Fill governance_overhead_review. Decide whether the current depth remains necessary or whether later phases should use a flatter structure with the same global guardrails.
Exit rule: Do not remove global no-go rules. Only the phase granularity may be flattened, and only after a recorded retrospective.

Trigger: Before preparing Phase 3.6 or Phase 4.1, or any wrapper around BoundaryOperatorProfile / ExecBoundaryMatrix.
Required input: BoundaryOperatorProfile shape; Operational/ExecComplex consumer needs; import direction; generator policy.
Required updates: Fill consumer_contract_direction. Phase 3.6 records the exported shape; Phase 4.1 records how it consumes the shape without reconstructing Schur/DtN internals.
Exit rule: Phase 3 may not import Phase 4. Phase 4 may not duplicate generator/Schur/DtN logic; it consumes the Phase-3 profile contract.

Trigger: Any phase that interprets IdealThread, CutSpec.full, Schur recursion, DtN boundary matrices, or string-like analogies.
Required input: Code references for IdealThread singleton carrier/family/coarsen lemmas; CutSpec.full carrier theorem; current generated boundary source; planned consumer shape.
Required updates: Record whether the phase consumes thread-scalar route, full-boundary route, or generated intermediate route. Mark analogical string language comparison-only unless a theorem gives a formal CNNA object.
Exit rule: No full matrix/operator, discriminant, spectrum, or physical interpretation may be promoted from the thread route unless the route and lifting theorem are explicitly present and code-checked.

Trigger: A phase attempts to relate Cayley-Dickson/Hurwitz algebra to arithmetic discriminant, CM order, norm, or transfermatrix data through Schur complementation.
Required input: Closed S05/BR06 horizontal boundary route, closed O05/BR07 vertical arithmetic-shadow route, Transfermatrix objects TM0-TM2, CD7 Hurwitz boundary, and explicit module manifest for the bridge.
Required updates: Create/update BR08/BR09/CD8 proof dossiers; record commutation/naturality target, failure modes, concrete finite instance traversal, and no-input-backflow audit.
Exit rule: The bridge is either proved as a theorem over derived objects, split into smaller prerequisite lemmas, or marked blocked. It may not feed earlier phases unless a separate backreaction/input handoff explicitly permits it.

Trigger: Whenever Eisenstein/Gauss b-ary tree geometry, angle profiles, CM points, j-invariants, or L_max sweeps are planned or implemented.
Required input: code-defined branching object/API; L_max set; embedding kind; angle profile; EXEC-2 boundary status; source objects L04/L06/O05/M05 when j/CM comparisons are used.
Required updates: Record static/dynamic branch rows, concrete finite instance traversal, operational vs mirror fields, expected executable witness, and mathlib/noncomputable complications. Add or update L09/L10/M08/BR10 dossiers.
Exit rule: The test may close only if all rows consume the canonical generator/branching API, no duplicate tree generator is introduced, operational assertions are rational/integer/finite, and mirror-only CM/j comparisons cannot feed generator semantics.

Trigger: Before implementing or promoting any operational computable path, arithmetic wrapper, generator extension, lattice test, or refactor that claims computation is preserved.
Required input: Canonical generator/branching API; finite instance parameters; L_max/branch-mode rows; expected executable witness; optional sentinel/comparison rows; mathlib/noncomputable risk log.
Required updates: Fill O07 or a consumer of O07; update concrete_instance_traversal, expected_executable_witness, mathlib_complication_log, and no-go guardrails. Separate generic rows from sentinel rows.
Exit rule: The phase cannot close on hand-picked CM/Eisenstein/Gauss examples alone. At least one generic row family must traverse the actual computable path, or a documented failure/backjump is required.

Workflow: Regime-typed Heegner/CM finite test matrix workflow
Trigger: Whenever finite computation tests, Eisenstein/Gauss sentinel rows, Heegner/class-number-one targets, CM/j targets, or noOuter/HasOuter regime claims are planned or implemented.
Required input: O07 generic finite rows; derived regime split; code-defined generator/branching API; L_max rows; EXEC-2 boundary; class-number data if Heegner interpretation is claimed.
Required updates: Fill O08/N18/BR11/BR12 dossiers; record regime tag, projection/complement-family status, generic-vs-sentinel status, operational witness, and mirror/comparison columns. Update mathlib/noncomputable log for any class-number/CM dependency.
Exit rule: The phase cannot close if h(D)=1 or a Heegner/j target is used to infer noOuter closure. Generic rows must exist; noOuter-root sentinel rows and HasOuter/projection rows must be distinguished explicitly.

Trigger: Any phase introduces scalar data beyond rational ExecComplex, uses mathlib Complex/Real/NumberField/QuadraticAlgebra, or proposes a Cayley-Dickson-derived C-layer.
Required input: O00/LEG20/LEG31, module manifest, reuse audit, expected executable witness, mathlib/noncomputable complication log, and CD noncircularity status if CD is involved.
Required updates: Update LEG31/LEG32, O09/O10/BR13/BR14/CD9 dossiers; classify each scalar as rational ExecComplex, exact quadratic schema, proof-carrying D-instantiation, integer/rational invariant, or analytic mirror-only. Record whether a new module is justified or existing module semantics suffice.
Exit rule: No new scalar layer may close without concrete finite instance traversal and proof-carrying projection back to allowed operational invariants. General Complex/Real and CD C-surfaces cannot become generator inputs, and exact quadratic instances require D-source provenance.

Workflow: Derived discriminant-indexed exact quadratic scalar workflow
Trigger: Any phase creates or consumes exact quadratic coordinates, theta, sqrt(D)-like notation, CM tau coordinates, quadratic-field/order coordinates, or lattice rows requiring algebraic irrational data.
Required input: O09 schema, LEG32/GNG19, proof-carrying D-source from O05/O06 or equivalent ledger, module manifest/reuse audit, mathlib/noncomputable complication log.
Required updates: Update O09/O10/BR14/BR13 dossiers; record whether the phase is schema-only, D-instantiation, invariant projection, or mirror comparison. Record concrete finite D-ledger rows and allowed invariant exports.
Exit rule: No phase may close with a free sqrt(D), an axiom, or an analytic Complex/CD source for theta. If D is unavailable or semantically blue, backjump to the discriminant-source correction rather than patching the scalar consumer.

Workflow: Phase-path dependency and object placement audit
Trigger: Before any implementation phase after Phase 4, or when moving/wrapping an object from N/L/M/SP/P/TM/BR/CD groups.
Required input: Object register, source-object list, target module manifest, dependency-depth audit, and current phase-path classification.
Required updates: Update phase title, object Phase field, proof dossier, scratchpad dependency_depth_audit, consumer_contract_direction, and no-go notes. Record whether the object belongs to Path A, Path B, or a late explicit bridge.
Exit rule: Phase may close only if its title, object group and dependencies agree. Finite spectral/physical consumers cannot be blocked behind modular/j/analytic phases unless a proof-carrying bridge says so.

Trigger: Whenever a phase is added, split, merged, moved, or whenever an object Phase field changes.
Required input: Phase hierarchy; object register; direct object ownership map; child-phase scope; moved/audit/closeout status.
Required updates: Update phase Object link policy, Object link explanation, Child object scope, object Phase fields, proof dossiers and phase table generator if needed.
Exit rule: The generated phase table may not contain an unexplained empty Linked objects cell. Direct ownership is never inferred from child scope; child-scope summaries are display-only.

Trigger: Whenever a phase introduces or consumes dynamics, spectra, operator algebra, geometry, arithmetic, stochasticity, open-system language, unitarity, AQFT/physics interpretation, or environment/complement effects.
Required input: POST1/LEG35/GNG22; source-object chain from IDEAL_infty formal background to subsystem/cut/approximant, boundary, regime, complement families and Schur/DtN or later certified bridge.
Required updates: Update object proof dossier, scratchpad source_to_cnna_mapping, dependency_depth_audit, no-go guardrails, and consumer-contract direction. Mark comparison-only interpretations explicitly and prohibit them from supplying definitions.
Exit rule: A phase may close only if non-subsystem structures are shown as derived consequences or comparison-only interpretations. Any primitive import of dynamics/physics/arithmetic/geometry/environment forces a backjump to the natural subsystem/complement generating layer.

Trigger: Before a Lean implementation phase starts, or when the user asks for the next semantically connected phases to be checked with minimal cognitive/tooling overhead.
Required input: Active phase, next semantic phase window, object register, scratchpad, module manifest, static findings, import/declaration inventory, and current pre-implementation-check ledger.
Required updates: Generate a compact implementation packet and avoid full PDF/declaration dumps unless the phase-check or audit-full mode explicitly requires them. Record G/Y/O/R thought-experiment results, predecessor assumptions, proof risk, and any red addressing phase proposals.
Exit rule: Lean coding may start only from a compact packet that names relevant modules, producer/consumer objects, red blockers, orange predecessor assumptions, yellow active implementability, forbidden shortcuts, and comparison-only theory boundaries.

Workflow: Local theory context versus end-theory comparison workflow
Trigger: Whenever a phase or module mentions established mathematical/physical theory, mathlib background, or a large target theory such as AQFT/OQS/modular forms/CM/Moonshine.
Required input: Module imports, declared CNNA objects, object proof dossiers, local background-theory context, and end-theory comparison ledger.
Required updates: Classify local established theory as formalization/proof-method/library context; classify large end theories only as comparison/target/consistency horizons. Record that neither role is an ontic CNNA generator unless derived internally.
Exit rule: A module may use established mathematics as formal language, but a CNNA object may close only if its producer is internal provenance. Large end theories remain comparison-only until CNNA derives a bridge object.

Workflow: DB-REVIEW database-ready documentation and expert-review workflow
Trigger: Whenever the YAML schema, generated views, phase/object records, proof dossiers, theory/reference markings, online/interactive documentation, or database/export plans are edited.
Required input: Lean-code data timestamp; YAML master; phase/object/proof-dossier records; status legend; global no-go guardrails GNG23-GNG24; local theory context/end-theory comparison ledgers when applicable.
Required updates: Keep Lean as primary truth system, generated views as secondary, and expert review as mandatory. Record whether a row is database-ready-later, review-required, blue-audit-needed, lean-primary, or generated-secondary when those semantics matter. Preserve round-trip-friendly IDs and explicit phase/object/review links.
Exit rule: No generated documentation may close by declaring semantic infallibility or 100-percent truth. Missing blue/reference markings must remain visible as audit debt, and database/export work must not become a CNNA-generative input.

Trigger: User asks for documentation about a specific object/topic, or a phase closeout requires documenting only the changed context rather than regenerating the whole PDF for human review.
Required input: Natural-language query or curated profile id; current Single-YAML-Master; regenerated generated_exports/*.tsv; optional slug.
Required updates: Run scripts/generate_tables.py first; run scripts/generate_context_doc.py with query/profile; package context_docs/<slug>/ manifest, section summary, TeX/PDF, and compile log. Add/adjust curated profile terms in YAML if the topic is recurring.
Exit rule: Every major documentation section is present in the context packet; each section records matched/total row counts; output states generated-secondary status, Lean-primary rule, and expert-review requirement.

Workflow: Semantic module consolidation and compatibility-preserving refactor workflow
Trigger: Before new substantive Lean code after governance closure, or whenever the user asks whether semantically related modules should be merged, compacted, facaded, or kept separate.
Required input: LEG10 module manifest; LEG11 reuse ledger; LEG5 consumption map; repo_inventory/raw_export modules/import_edges/symbol references; candidate file list; direct code spot-checks; current public/API and proof-dossier records.
Required updates: Update LEG36, code_audit findings, reuse_duplication_ledger, scratchpad refactor fields, proposed modules, duplicate-risk symbols, natural-layer placement, facade/compatibility plan, and regression metrics. Set downstream content phases to orange if this gate must precede them.
Exit rule: No Lean merge may close without old-module facade or explicit migration plan, import graph delta, declaration/consumer delta, local full-build evidence, and proof that semantic/provenance auditability is preserved or improved.

Workflow: Root artifact retention and generated-output cleanup workflow
Trigger: Before packaging a tool release, after full/context documentation generation, or when root PDFs/TeX/Markdown reports accumulate.
Required input: Current Repository root file list; Single-YAML-Master; README.md; generated tables/exports; optional generated_docs/full output; archive/reports policy.
Required updates: Keep README.md in root; move historical Markdown reports to archive/reports/; delete disposable root PDF/TeX/LaTeX auxiliary files; ensure full documentation is generated under generated_docs/full/ on demand; update DBR8/GNG27 if classification changes.
Exit rule: Project root contains no retained generated PDF/TeX/LaTeX build products and no active historical Markdown reports beyond README.md. All retained source data remains in YAML/scripts/tables/generated_exports/repo_inventory/repo_snapshot as appropriate.

Workflow: Root artifact retention and Lean/Lake build-entry preservation workflow
Trigger: Before packaging a tool release, after root cleanup, or when Lean/Lake build-control files are added, removed, or moved.
Required input: Package root file list; CNNA.lean; lakefile.lean; lake-manifest.json; lean-toolchain; Repository/repo_snapshot/CNNA source tree; README.md; cleanup script policy.
Required updates: Ensure package-root Lean/Lake project-control files are preserved; ensure generated docs/reports remain outside active root; update DBR10/GNG28 and README if the build-entry surface changes.
Exit rule: Package root contains the source/control files needed for local Lake build and contains no retained generated PDF/TeX/LaTeX byproducts or historical active reports.

Workflow: Root license, README and credit-surface workflow
Trigger: Before packaging a tool release, when licensing/credits change, or when README files appear outside the package root.
Required input: Package root file list; README.md; LICENSE; license_notice table; metadata editor/assistant/minimum_assistant_requirement; full/context documentation generators; cleanup policy.
Required updates: Keep README.md and LICENSE in package root; remove duplicate active README files in subdirectories; update license_notice/Stats/PDF author metadata; update DBR11/GNG29 and README if credits, license scope, or tested assistant setup changes.
Exit rule: Exactly one maintained README.md exists at package root; root LICENSE exists; generated full/context PDFs expose Editor and Assistant credits; package contains no root PDF/TeX/LaTeX byproducts.

Trigger: When full or context documentation layout requirements change, especially section-boundary/page-navigation rules for review PDFs.
Required input: Full-document generator, context-document generator, Master YAML metadata, representative full/context PDF outputs, and the user-facing README command surface.
Required updates: Patch generator preambles or layout macros; keep disposable generated TeX/PDF out of the package root; regenerate tables and representative PDFs; update DBR12/GNG30/SCF49 and README if user commands or layout expectations change.
Exit rule: The full documentation PDF and at least one context documentation PDF compile successfully, their generated TeX contains the section-pagebreak hook, and no Lean source or context-matching semantics are changed.

Workflow: Phase status release and red-lock workflow
Trigger: Before adding phases, changing a phase status, advancing the active cursor, or preparing a Lean-derivation implementation prompt.
Required input: phase row; implementation_scratchpad.phase_type; prerequisite object IDs; proof dossier status; pre-implementation check result; current active cursor; relevant static findings and guardrails.
Required updates: If phase_type=derivation and the phase is not closed, active, or explicitly implementable-after-predecessors, set it red. To release red, add a pre-implementation check that references already-derived Lean objects and sets yellow only for the unique active cursor or orange for later implementable phases. Keep white only for non-direct-Lean refactor/tool/infrastructure/documentation/comparison phases.
Exit rule: Generated tables pass validation: exactly one yellow phase; zero white derivation phases; red derivation phases carry lock language; any yellow/orange release has explicit prerequisite evidence or a corresponding pre-implementation check record.

Workflow: Documentation section explanation and appendix workflow
Trigger: When a documentation section/table is confusing, redundant, or appears to duplicate a primary control surface.
Required input: Full-document section order, context-document SECTION_ORDER, static table paragraphs, Master YAML sections, generated exports, and user review feedback.
Required updates: Add or tighten static generator-owned explanations, keep table rows dynamic, move narrow technical tables to appendix when they are not primary reading surfaces, and update DBR/GNG/static finding records if semantics change.
Exit rule: A reader can identify what each section/table displays, where the data comes from, why it is shown, and which table is authoritative for phase release. No dynamic prose rows are added only to explain static section purpose.

Workflow: Pre-substantive-code readiness and proof-gap audit workflow
Trigger: Before starting any new substantive Lean/content implementation, especially before activating 1.1.1.2 or any later red locked derivation phase.
Required input: Current full zip; repo_snapshot/CNNA source tree; repo_inventory/raw_export module/import/declaration inventory; generated_exports consumption/object/proof/phase tables; local build result if refactor is executed.
Required updates: Fill LEG37 source-intake ledger; create LEG38 neutral naming/facade plan; execute only approved LEG39 refactor package; regenerate inventory and close LEG40; issue LEG41 release proof or create upstream red blockers.
Exit rule: Exactly one active Y phase remains. No new substantive Lean code begins until LEG41 references closed derived-only/verified objects and the stable neutral API/source surface.

Workflow: Provider-neutral SQL public read-only mirror and web-frontend workflow
Trigger: When the database/web-publication sidechain is activated or when a provider-neutral SQL database mirror, public read-only API, cnna-project.net frontend, reviewer annotation layer or direct importer is designed.
Required input: Master YAML, generated TSV exports, repo_inventory/raw_export, all three timestamp-complete version pairs (lean_data_version/lean_data_timestamp, planning_data_version/planning_data_timestamp, tool_infrastructure_version/tool_infrastructure_timestamp), source zip name, YAML/repo inventory/tool hashes, public read-only database endpoint/credential for read-only frontend, local admin write credential key only via .env for admin importer if/when executed, and CEX16 red-failure-history schema when CI history is exposed. lean_data_version is the Lean toolchain/API level; lean_data_timestamp is the Lean source/data snapshot time.
Required updates: Add or update DBR15-DBR20, schema/import/read-only access policy/frontend contracts, snapshot/checksum records, read-only grant/access tests, public view definitions, README/manual commands, archive report and failure-history views if red CI history is public. Never store secrets in repo/chat.
Exit rule: A DB mirror phase may close only when every public current row traces to a snapshot_id with all three version+timestamp pairs, public read-only access is read-only through api.* views, internal/admin schemas are closed, admin write credential remains server/local-admin only, and a YAML/export roundtrip or import report is archived. If CI history is exposed, red failure rows must be visibly separated from green current rows and linked to repair phases. The Lean pair must remain asymmetric when appropriate: toolchain version is not the source snapshot timestamp.
Change note: P1.40 workflow-coherence audit: clarified no-build vs red-build states, delta-PDF closeout, red-history API/deployment dependencies, and added anti-self-sabotage workflow guardrail.

Trigger: When generated full/context documentation readability or typography scaling changes are requested.
Required input: Full-document generator, context-document generator, table generator, representative full/context PDF outputs, generated TeX audit and README command surface.
Required updates: Patch generator-level size profile, regenerate tables, compile representative PDFs, render sample pages, update DBR21/GNG35/SCF54 and README if user-facing layout expectations change.
Exit rule: Generated full and context PDFs compile with the intended typography profile, generated TeX shows the larger size profile, representative pages render without obvious clipping/black-glyph failure, and no Lean/YAML semantics change.

Trigger: Whenever a phase or object is created, moved, reclassified, exported to DB, or rendered in a phase/object table.
Required input: Declared Context class from context_class_taxonomy plus phase_type/object provenance/status fields.
Required updates: Master YAML phase/object row; proof dossier if object; scratchpad if phase; generated tables and TSV exports.
Exit rule: No blank Context class; class must not contradict handoff/access rights or generated table semantics.

Workflow: Object patch and supersession lifecycle workflow
Trigger: A Lean/planning/tool object is patched, semantically replaced, or receives a corrected canonical description/dossier.
Required input: Old object ID, old dossier pointer, new or corrected object ID/dossier, patching phase ID, reason for replacement, snapshot/version timestamps and affected consumers.
Required updates: Do not overwrite the old object row. Set old Object lifecycle state to superseded, fill Lifecycle successor/current object and Lifecycle note, keep old proof dossier readable, create or update the successor object/dossier, link the successor phase to the old phase/object, and record consumer migration duties.
Exit rule: The lifecycle chain is acyclic, the old object remains queryable as historical provenance, the new/current object has a current dossier pointer, and no phase is marked outdated.

Trigger: A Lean declaration/module/object or planning object is renamed without intending to preserve the old name as current canonical API.
Required input: Old name/ID, new name/ID, rename phase, module/declaration diff evidence or planning diff, affected imports/consumers, old and new dossier pointers.
Required updates: Retain the old object row as renamed-alias, point it to the new/current object, preserve the old dossier as historical alias evidence, create/update the new object row and dossier, and record any migration links in module/object registers.
Exit rule: Explorer/SQL views can resolve old aliases to the current object while still displaying original provenance; no historical row is deleted or silently merged.

Workflow: Object deletion or retirement lifecycle workflow
Trigger: An object/declaration/module is removed from Lean or planning, or intentionally retired as no longer needed.
Required input: Removed object ID, deletion/retirement phase, deletion evidence, reason, downstream consumers, replacement object if any, snapshot/version timestamps.
Required updates: Retain the object row with Object lifecycle state deleted-retired, keep its dossier, fill successor if a replacement exists or explain terminal retirement in Lifecycle note, and create migration/blocker tasks for affected consumers.
Exit rule: The object no longer appears as current in Explorer projections but remains visible in history/provenance queries; successor chains terminate in a current object or explicit terminal retirement.

Trigger: A later correction/refactor/patch changes or replaces an object that was produced or consumed by an earlier phase.
Required input: Old phase ID, new patch/successor phase ID, affected objects, reason for patch, version/timestamp evidence and migration obligations.
Required updates: Never mark the old phase outdated. Keep it as a historical implementation event; link the new phase to the old phase via patch/successor relation in phase notes, object lifecycle rows and scratchpad/dossier references.
Exit rule: Both phases remain independently queryable; the new phase explains the correction and the old phase remains part of historical provenance without being treated as current object validity.

Trigger: A question arises whether evidence belongs under a phase scratchpad, object dossier, lifecycle ledger or generated history entry.
Required input: Entity type, evidence type, target object/phase IDs, version/timestamp and whether the evidence describes implementation execution, object validity, lifecycle migration or generated diff history.
Required updates: Store implementation process evidence in the phase scratchpad; store object validity/proof evidence in the object dossier; store patch/rename/delete/supersession evidence in object lifecycle fields and lifecycle ledger; store snapshot diffs in history records. Cross-link rather than duplicate or overwrite.
Exit rule: Generated views can reconstruct both the historical implementation path and the current object state without contradiction.

Trigger: Phase 21.0, LEG37, Explorer schema work or any public API/refactor phase needs to claim that the current Lean declaration surface is fully understood.
Required input: Current Lean snapshot timestamp, lean toolchain version, repo_inventory/raw_export, declaration_consumption_records.generated.tsv, object register, proof dossiers, lifecycle ledger and direct source scan evidence.
Required updates: For every Lean declaration, record exactly one declaration_kind, semantic_role, triviality_tier, significance/documentation policy, lifecycle_state, public_surface_status and owning CNNA object or explicit non-object bucket. Unknown/manual-review values may be stored only as open blockers with natural-origin follow-up action.
Exit rule: LEG37/CEX0/21.0 may close only when all declarations have total classification or every unresolved declaration is explicitly routed to a red/orange upstream phase; consumption class alone never suffices.

Trigger: An automatic scanner marks a declaration trivial/nontrivial based on rfl, projection shape, theorem/structure keyword, module path or consumer count.
Required input: Automatic candidate label, proof/body/consumer evidence, source object or owner bucket, handoff/boundary/API relevance and manual reviewer note when the automatic label is overridden.
Required updates: Store both the automatic candidate and the curated final triviality/significance/documentation decision. In particular, rfl/projection declarations that encode handoff, boundary or fundamental definitional contracts may be promoted; ledger/status structures may be demoted when they carry no mathematical payload.
Exit rule: Explorer and proofbook views render the curated final class plus override reason when present; no heuristic-only triviality class may release a declaration to public-final or exclude it from required documentation.

Workflow: Lean declaration owner-bucket and lifecycle workflow
Trigger: A declaration is added, patched, renamed, deleted, retired, superseded, or cannot be attached to a registered object.
Required input: Declaration name, module path, previous/current snapshot IDs, object/dossier candidate, successor/predecessor relation if any, reason for non-object bucket if not owned by a CNNA object, and public-surface status.
Required updates: Attach the declaration to exactly one owning object or explicit non-object bucket, assign lifecycle state, preserve old names as historical rows when renamed/deleted/retired, and update successor/current pointers without overwriting old evidence.
Exit rule: Generated history can reconstruct both current declaration state and original provenance; lifecycle/successor chains remain acyclic and no active consumer points to a deleted-retired or superseded declaration except through an explicit historical/facade edge.

Trigger: An uploaded legacy/original roadmap contains content not yet represented in the Single-YAML-Master planning state.
Required input: Roadmap source file, current master YAML, object register, phase table, scratchpads, dossiers, external-source cross-check, guardrails and lifecycle records.
Required updates: Copy the source into archive/source_materials when useful; add only missing source hierarchy, gate, chain, migration, de-seeding, register, context or future-target obligations. Route accepted items to explicit phases/objects/dossiers/cross-check rows; mark rejected/deferred items by reason. Do not import roadmap claims as axioms or checked Lean facts.
Exit rule: Every accepted roadmap obligation has a phase/object/dossier/cross-check row or an explicit no-action reason; Lean code remains unchanged unless a separate implementation phase is executed.

Trigger: A Seed, Scaffold, Window, Boundary-first or legacy module is proposed for current canonical API or de-seeded naming.
Required input: Source map, derived-only gate evidence, notation/readability binding, free-parameter audit, old-path regression oracle, importgraph cycle check, rollback snapshot and affected object lifecycle rows.
Required updates: Create/close the natural source-map gate before renaming; keep old object/module as regression or lifecycle-retired provenance; update object lifecycle/dossiers and module manifest; do not patch the consumer phase.
Exit rule: The new current object has source-map and gate evidence; the old path remains queryable; importgraph is acyclic; rollback snapshot exists; no free legacy parameter remains hidden.

Trigger: A roadmap chain sheet identifies a multi-step dependency path such as modular B core, ParameterClosure or D/E bridge.
Required input: Chain definitions, prerequisites, proof/claim text, target modules, current plan coverage and missing source objects.
Required updates: Represent the chain as a phase/object/gate group with explicit predecessor order; add guardrails where shortcuts are forbidden; mark as reference-only until CNNA source objects close.
Exit rule: The chain is visible in phase order, object register, cross-check rows and scratchpad obligations without implying that the chain is already proved.

Trigger: A legacy REALOQS source archive is uploaded or re-scanned.
Required input: versioned source archive; scan timestamp; current planning package; target focus such as AQFT/PillarB
Required updates: Archive source, generate module/declaration metrics, record noncomputable/simp/sorry scan, add external-source rows, update RLC ledgers and lifecycle pointers.
Exit rule: A scan report exists and every accepted finding is routed to reference/migration ledgers, not to CNNA truth.

Trigger: A legacy AQFT declaration/module is considered for CNNA reuse.
Required input: legacy module path; declaration list; dependency/import context; current CNNA source objects and handoff contract
Required updates: Classify as target-profile, semantic-rewrite, mathlib replacement, regression-only, direct-port-forbidden, reject/defer; update object dossier and phase gate.
Exit rule: No implementation starts until each accepted target has a CNNA source-map, neutral target module and forbidden-dependency audit.

Trigger: Legacy declarations need Explorer visibility or migration analysis.
Required input: RLC0 scan data, CEX11 taxonomy, current CNNA declaration inventory, lifecycle ledger
Required updates: Map each legacy declaration to owner bucket, semantic role, target CNNA object/phase or reject/defer bucket; mark lifecycle and edge type as legacy/reference.
Exit rule: Explorer may show the legacy node only if its non-CNNA epistemic status and edge semantics are explicit.

Trigger: A REALOQS example/meta proof is useful as a future test.
Required input: legacy example/meta module, target CNNA phase, expected old behavior, source-map risk
Required updates: Create regression expectation without importing code; quarantine example; link to future derived replacement and rollback/compatibility check.
Exit rule: Regression harness is recorded as test evidence only, not as a source object or closure proof.

Trigger: A phase/object claims derived-only closure, arithmetic/physics seed success, or green status for a nontrivial path.
Required input: Structural skeleton; local proof obligations; generated data source; operational smoke path; verification evidence; current Lean data timestamp.
Required updates: Record skeleton, proof modules, generated-data origin, smoke path, and verification result in scratchpad/dossier; mark claim yellow/orange/red until the complete path is shown.
Exit rule: The phase/object can close only when the operational generated-data path is explicit and the dossier distinguishes skeleton success from real data validation.

Trigger: Implementation, proof search or pre-implementation thought experiment discovers a missing structure in a downstream phase. Includes red CI failures produced by the active phase branch/PR.
Required input: Failing consumer, missing structure description, candidate source layers, rejected patch sites, dependency graph, proof/audit reason for the selected origin.
Required updates: Mark downstream phase red; add static finding; create/activate the earliest natural upstream phase; document why the structure belongs there and not at the consumer. For red CI, also expose the failure through the visible failure-history channel and link it to the generated repair/localization phase.
Exit rule: Downstream phase can reopen only after the upstream natural-origin phase closes and lifecycle/provenance links are updated.
Change note: P1.39 timestamp-bounded delta record: CI red-path visibility plus delta-PDF output policy.

Trigger: The user asks whether future phases are implementable or which positive path phase should become active.
Required input: Current phase/object table, dependencies, linked objects, module seams, no-go guardrails, implementation packet, known red blockers.
Required updates: Simulate implementation pressure rather than only checking listed dependencies; record hidden dependencies, route blockers upstream, and assign G/Y/O/R status with evidence.
Exit rule: A phase is yellow only when the thought experiment finds no missing source object/consumer contract; orange only with explicit predecessor closures; red if an upstream natural-origin blocker exists.

Trigger: CNNA planning complexity exceeds linear-document manageability or a new visual/database presentation is proposed.
Required input: Master YAML, generated exports, stable IDs, typed nodes/edges, lifecycle status, declaration classification, external-reference/hypothesis markers.
Required updates: Treat YAML/SQL/Explorer as epistemic control and review surfaces; keep PDF/TeX as generated renderings; ensure all Explorer status displays are backed by machine-readable fields.
Exit rule: Explorer/UI may present the project only when it preserves Lean/YAML truth separation, edge semantics, lifecycle status, and review/hypothesis markers.

Workflow: Pillar co-development and acyclic handoff workflow
Trigger: A later plan requires B, C, D and E to be developed in a mutually constraining way from Pillar A.
Required input: Pillar-specific source objects, A->X handoffs, input handoff rights, backreaction channels, graph edge types, cycle checks.
Required updates: Represent co-development as explicit acyclic handoff/backreaction windows; do not replace the partial order by informal mutual dependency.
Exit rule: The plan may show co-development visually only when proof-producing provenance remains acyclic and all backreaction enters through declared input handoffs.

Trigger: The assistant or an automated script is asked to modify the CNNA GitHub repository rather than only an uploaded zip.
Required input: Repository full name, current default-branch SHA, branch-protection rules, GitHub App/API permissions, intended phase/object scope and active no-go guardrails.
Required updates: Create or verify a backup tag/branch for the current default-branch SHA before any mutable work; create a work branch; commit changes there; open a PR; run required CI; record backup ref, work branch, PR, commit SHA and CI status in the planning/export evidence. If backup ref creation or verification fails, stop before work-branch mutation.
Exit rule: Normal path exits only through PR plus green required checks. Direct main writes are blocked except explicit emergency override with recorded backup, reason and rollback path. No automated repository write may proceed without a recoverable backup ref for the parent commit it modifies.
Change note: P1.40 workflow-coherence audit: clarified no-build vs red-build states, delta-PDF closeout, red-history API/deployment dependencies, and added anti-self-sabotage workflow guardrail.

Workflow: CI snapshot-artifact and Webhosting-MySQL import workflow
Trigger: A green Lean/inventory build should update the public Explorer or MySQL-backed production/current mirror; red builds must instead route to CEX15/CEX16 and publish visible failure history without current swap.
Required input: Pinned lean-toolchain/lake-manifest, Master YAML, generated exports, repo inventory, snapshot_manifest schema, Webhosting import endpoint or server-local importer, DB credentials held outside public code.
Required updates: Run lake build and scans; generate YAML/JSON/TSV/SQL artifacts plus hashes. On green CI, upload artifact, import only into staging, validate row/hash/provenance coverage, swap current read-only mirror atomically, and archive backup/validation reports. On red CI, stop before production/current upload/import, emit failure evidence to the phase-state controller, publish visible read-only failure history through CEX16 and link the generated repair predecessor.
Exit rule: Green deployment exits only if previous current snapshot remains rollbackable, public API is read-only, every row carries commit/toolchain/hash provenance and failed imports leave the old current snapshot active. Red CI exits through CEX15 repair-phase generation plus CEX16 failure-history publication, not production/current deployment.
Change note: P1.39 timestamp-bounded delta record: CI red-path visibility plus delta-PDF output policy.

Trigger: A GitHub Actions Lean/Lake build and declared scan pipeline completes for the active phase branch or PR.
Required input: active phase_id; current work branch/PR; backup ref; commit SHA; GitHub Actions run/job conclusion; Lean build logs; scanner/artifact manifest; current phase/object/proof/scratchpad registers; dependency graph; last green snapshot.
Required updates: If CI is red, block merge and production/current deploy/import, set the attempted active phase to R, record failure evidence, publish a visible read-only failure-history snapshot, and create or activate a predecessor repair phase at the natural source layer with object-link policy, dependencies, scratchpad and proof-dossier stubs. If CI is green, record build/scanner/artifact evidence, set the active phase to G only when closeout fields are complete, update linked object/dossier/scratchpad rows, and advance exactly one active cursor to the next dependency-complete phase.
Exit rule: Red exit leaves no production SQL/Explorer current update and no merge, but does leave a visible failure-history snapshot and repair predecessor as the active follow-up if dependencies are known. Green exit leaves the completed phase G, evidence complete, public mirrors updated only through validated snapshot import, and exactly one next active phase selected.
Change note: P1.39 timestamp-bounded delta record: CI red-path visibility plus delta-PDF output policy.

Workflow: Local archival and rollback workflow for automated snapshots
Trigger: A repository or public database snapshot becomes a release candidate or public Explorer state.
Required input: Git backup tag/branch or commit SHA, workflow artifact, SQL dump/snapshot, snapshot_manifest.json, validation report and release timestamp; for red CI, red_failure_snapshot.json and repair-phase link as history artifacts rather than production/current release artifacts.
Required updates: Create downloadable/locally archivable git bundle or mirror-clone instruction; store SQL dump or generated SQL artifact for green release candidates; record manifest hashes; document restore path for Git and MySQL separately. Preserve red failure snapshots in history/archive without promoting them to current production state.
Exit rule: A release snapshot is not considered deployment-hardened until Git state, generated artifacts and database mirror state can each be restored independently. A red failure snapshot is considered review-visible only if archived or queryable as history while last green current remains rollbackable.
Change note: P1.40 workflow-coherence audit: clarified no-build vs red-build states, delta-PDF closeout, red-history API/deployment dependencies, and added anti-self-sabotage workflow guardrail.

Trigger: A Lean/scan CI run fails for the active phase branch/PR and the failure affects phase-state transition.
Required input: commit SHA; GitHub Actions run/job IDs; active phase_id; failed job names; sanitized log/artifact pointers; phase_state_transition_manifest.json; generated repair-phase ID/link; last green snapshot ID/hash; no-current-swap evidence.
Required updates: Generate red_failure_snapshot.json or equivalent; insert/read-only expose a failure-history row/API view; link the failure to the generated predecessor repair phase or localization gate; preserve last green production/current snapshot; scrub secrets and avoid publishing excessive logs.
Exit rule: The red failure is visible to reviewers and Explorer/history consumers, but no merge, production/current MySQL swap or green-snapshot publication occurs. The failure artifact is generated-secondary evidence, not a truth source.
Change note: P1.39 timestamp-bounded delta record: CI red-path visibility plus delta-PDF output policy.

Trigger: A planning update changes a bounded set of YAML rows under one planning_data_timestamp/change timestamp, but no full-release or full-audit checkpoint is requested or explicitly triggered by the user.
Required input: planning_data_version; planning_data_timestamp; per-row Change version; per-row Change timestamp; changed-row section/key list; generated TSV exports; optional context-slice query for the same change version.
Required updates: Mark every changed row with the same Change version and Change timestamp; regenerate TSV/table exports; emit a delta PDF containing only matching changed rows/sections and a matched-row export bundle; do not compile or present the full documentation PDF as the default artifact.
Exit rule: The user receives the updated full ZIP plus a delta PDF. The delta PDF contains only records whose Change version/Change timestamp match the update set, with an explicit note that Lean remains primary and the delta is generated-secondary. Full PDF generation is optional, explicit or release/full-audit-gated and must not be silently substituted as the default bounded-change artifact.
Change note: P1.40 workflow-coherence audit: clarified no-build vs red-build states, delta-PDF closeout, red-history API/deployment dependencies, and added anti-self-sabotage workflow guardrail.

Workflow: Workflow coherence, precedence and anti-self-sabotage audit workflow
Trigger: Before adding, deleting or materially changing workflow_policy rows; before activating CI/DB/Explorer automation; after any user identifies a possible workflow conflict or self-blocking rule.
Required input: Complete workflow_policy section; global_no_go_guardrails; active phase table; object/proof/scratchpad registers; CI red/green state-machine objects CEX15-CEX16; database/read-only mirror policy; documentation-output policy; current latest_change_overview.
Required updates: Classify every affected workflow by state transition domain (preparation, implementation, build evidence, red failure, green closeout, DB mirror, backup/rollback, documentation). Check for contradictory exits, missing predecessor dependencies, hidden red paths, false-green paths, direct-main writes, production/current swaps on red, public write leaks and full-PDF churn. Record every contradiction as a static finding and patch the earliest workflow/source layer, not downstream consumers.
Exit rule: The workflow set passes only if missing-evidence, red-failure, green-success and documentation-delta states remain distinct; red failures are visible but never current production; green closeout requires evidence and exactly one active cursor; public DB/API remains read-only; backup/rollback precedes mutable repository or current-mirror changes; bounded planning changes emit delta PDFs by default.
Change note: P1.40 workflow-coherence audit: clarified no-build vs red-build states, delta-PDF closeout, red-history API/deployment dependencies, and added anti-self-sabotage workflow guardrail.
